nodes.name: When Executed by WF-001
nodes.notes: Input data mode = 'Accept all data'. In the exported JSON this is inputSource: 'passthrough' (LOWERCASE t — verified against a real n8n export; the camelCase 'passThrough' I previously authored is not the value n8n emits). Receives the full payload from WF-001 including binary.

parameters.query: SELECT settings FROM configuration.providers WHERE kind='ocr';

nodes.name: Load OCR Provider Settings
nodes.notes: Runtime endpoint/model/timeout (R-08). Bind CRIE Postgres credential.

nodes.name: Re-attach Binary (pattern)
nodes.notes: PATTERN — restore binary after a Postgres node that precedes a binary consumer. Any future SW workflow with Postgres-before-binary-consumer must apply this same re-attach-from-trigger step. See CRIE Workflow Engineering Guidelines.

parameters.url: ={{ $('Load OCR Provider Settings').first().json.settings.endpoint }}/documentintelligence/documentModels/{{ $('Load OCR Provider Settings').first().json.settings.model }}:analyze?api-version=2024-11-30

nodes.name: submit() — POST Analyze
nodes.notes: SUBMIT adapter (Azure). POST :analyze with the binary. fullResponse=true so the Operation-Location header is available. neverError=true so 202 is not treated as an error. Node-level retry handles transient 429/503 only — NOT operation progress. Auth: Generic Credential Type -> Header Auth (httpHeaderAuth). Credential must be re-bound in the n8n UI after import (n8n does not preserve credential IDs across imports).

nodes.name: submit() — capture pollUrl
nodes.notes: Captures the poll URL (Azure: Operation-Location) using provider config (settings.poll.pollUrlHeader), honors Retry-After for the first wait, and seeds the poll budget (maxAttempts / maxElapsedMs). A non-202 or missing header is a terminal submit failure.

nodes.name: poll() — wait
nodes.notes: Waits the interval computed by the poll adapter (honors Retry-After; default ~2s per Azure guidance).

nodes.name: poll() — GET operation
nodes.notes: GET the operation URL. fullResponse for Retry-After header. Node retry = transient faults only. Auth: Generic Credential Type -> Header Auth (httpHeaderAuth). Credential must be re-bound in the n8n UI after import (n8n does not preserve credential IDs across imports).

nodes.name: poll() — evaluate
nodes.notes: Normalises the provider poll response to {status,result} via config (statusPath, terminalSuccess/Failure, resultPath). Honors Retry-After, backs off on 429, increments the attempt counter, and enforces maxAttempts/maxElapsedMs so the loop can never run forever.

nodes.name: terminal?
nodes.notes: true -> terminal (succeeded/failed/timeout). false -> loop back to Wait and poll again. Schema is a verbatim copy of a real n8n IF v2 export (nested parameters.conditions object); only the expression/value were substituted.

nodes.name: succeeded?
nodes.notes: succeeded -> normalize(). failed/timeout -> ocr=null so WF-001's §154 OCR-fail path fires. Schema is a verbatim copy of a real n8n IF v2 export.

nodes.name: normalize() — Normalized OCR Model
nodes.notes: TEMPORARY verification log at line 1 (remove once confirmed). Reads $json as the canonical input: the IF gates (terminal? + succeeded?) already proved this item is the terminal successful poll. NO $('poll() — evaluate') history lookups remain.

nodes.name: shapeResult() — OCR failed
nodes.notes: Emits ocr=null + ocrError so WF-001's node-if-ocr routes to the §154 HUMAN_REVIEW path. Reads the item directly; no run-history lookup.
